content:
This piece was originally published via my newsletter.
We had another DeepSeek moment last week. A Chinese AI firm, Moonshot AI, launched a model (Kimi K3) that can compete with America’s elite frontier labs for a fraction of the cost. Yes, I’m referring to OpenAI, Anthropic, Google, etc.
You can read my DeepSeek piece about why I still think American frontier labs have a competitive edge, but what I want to focus on here is not geopolitics, technology, law, or economics. I want to focus on the founder of Moonshot AI — Zhilin Yang.
America educated him. As you likely read from the image above, his personal GitHub reads like an American success story — PhD from Carnegie Mellon and research at Google Brain and Meta AI.
That startup he said he was working on? That became Moonshot AI based in Beijing. And its new Kimi K3 model just rocked Silicon Valley’s world last week, along with the U.S. stock market.
The most recent credible challenge to American AI labs came from someone American institutions trained.
If you listen to his PhD advisor from Carnegie Mellon, Ruslan Salakhutdinov, Yang always planned to return to China and build his own company…
